self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can improve future Engine source adaptation quality by letting later planning stages consume typed outputs from earlier stages instead of recomputing everything from the same flat input.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP Architecture case opens one bounded extraction-to-adaptation chaining slice in engine/directive-engine.ts rather than a broad pipeline rewrite.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP opener, the Engine planning pipeline constructed extraction, adaptation, improvement, proof, and integration plans independently from the same planningInput, and the ts-edge finding existed only as a parked STANDARD source case.
self_improvement.expected_effect: The first chained seam will make progressive stage-output planning real in Engine code and provide a concrete base for later bounded chaining work if it proves worthwhile.

decision.rationale: The parked ts-edge STANDARD result justifies one separate DEEP Architecture implementation slice to materialize the first progressive stage-output chaining seam in Engine code.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
